relations.subject: Black-headed woodpecker
relations.evidence: Black-headed woodpeckers are found in Cambodia, Laos, Myanmar, Thailand, and Vietnam. They are most commonly found in the mid-story and sub-canopy of deciduous and coniferous forests. They usually prefer dry dipterocarp forests, semi-evergreen forests, riverine forests, and savanna forests.{{Cite journal|last1=Zusi|first1=R
relations.object_summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

relations.subject: Black-headed woodpecker
relations.evidence: The black-headed woodpecker is currently classified as least concern, but populations are decreasing. This is mostly due to habitat destruction caused by human settlements and agricultural fields. In 2003, the black-headed woodpecker was declared a protected wildlife species by the Thai government.{{Cite web|date=2003|title=กฎกระทรวง ก ำหนดให้สัตว์ป่ำบางชนิดเป็นสัตว์ป่ำคุ้มครอง พ.ศ
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。
